Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails or list_emails with a query related to "Mehta" or "housing" or "UrbanNest" (or similar) to find the Dr. Mehta housing correspondence.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails or list_emails with a query related to "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "Patricia Langford" or "equipment" (or similar) to find the full Dr. Mehta relocation thread.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails or list_emails with a query related to "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "vendor" or "capacity" (or similar) to determine whether Swift Relocations has confirmed capacity for the Mehta equipment shipment.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails or list_emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" or "hazmat" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance inquiry details.
OE 5: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01) with a query related to "Fatimah" or by browsing all records to identify Fatimah Al-Rashidi's active relocations. Expected findings — active (In Progress) moves assigned to Fatimah:
- Dr. Renata Kovacs, Canopy Health, Nashville TN → Toronto ON, April 14–17 (international cross-border, customs/visa documentation)
- Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack Energy, Houston TX → Portland OR, April 17–28 (HAZMAT: Class 9 lithium-ion, split shipment, 11-day storage gap)
- Tomas Herrera, GreenStack Energy, Chicago IL → Austin TX, April 14–16
- Lena Kowalski, Mosaic Robotics, Detroit MI → Salt Lake City UT, April 22–24
- Nadia Okonjo, PivotPoint Labs, San Francisco CA → Austin TX, April 14–18 (H-1B visa holder)
- Raj Patel (audit note), NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL → Denver CO, April 10
Additionally note: no Airtable relocation record exists for Dr. Anand Mehta despite active CRM deal and Linear tickets — this is a tracking gap.
OE 6: Search CRM for the Canopy Health / Mehta deal using crm_search_contacts, crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with a query related to "Canopy" or "Mehta" (or similar). Expected findings: deal "Canopy Health — Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 2026)" at $12,170, stage in-progress, close date May 7, 2026. This is the first C-suite relocation for Canopy Health (MoveOps' longest-standing client since July 2024). Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto. Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi.
OE 7: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "housing" or "equipment" (or similar), or list issues for the proj_canopy project.
OE 8: Search Linear using linear_list_issues or linear_list_projects with a query related to "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" (or similar) to find the DOT Compliance Audit project (project_dot_compliance_audit).
OE 9: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Kovacs" or "Toronto" or "customs" or "BorderEase" (or similar), or search emails for Kovacs status updates.
OE 10: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages or search emails for messages related to "Zhao" or "Portland" or "storage" or "Pacific Crest" (or similar) to check the Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat shipment status.
OE 11: Look up Dr. Anand Mehta's contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with query "Mehta" or "Anand" (or similar). Expected finding: anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io.
OE 12: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with query "Chloe Vance" (or similar). Expected finding: chloe.vance@moveops.com, Slack user_id: moveops_chloe_vance (needed for tagging in the #operations message).
OE 13: Send a follow-up email using send_email from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com to anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io (CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and/or patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io) following up on the temporary housing options. Content should reference the three options sent earlier (particularly recommending Option B at The Pinnacle in Streeterville), note that the 48-hour hold deadline has passed and units may need to be re-confirmed with UrbanNest, and convey urgency given his May 5 start date is now 9 days away. Ask Dr. Mehta to confirm his preferred unit so that movers and equipment logistics can be finalized.
OE 14: Update stale tracking records. This should include the following:
- Add a Linear comment on ticket #343 (Temporary Housing) using linear_create_comment noting that Dr. Mehta did not respond by the April 25 deadline, the 48-hour hold has expired, follow-up email sent, and UrbanNest will need to re-confirm unit availability.
- Add a Linear comment on ticket #375 (Equipment) using linear_create_comment noting that Swift capacity confirmation from Greg Pallone is still pending and needs to be obtained urgently given the May 5 deadline.
- Update the Kovacs Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (record: recKovacsNashvilleToronto) to change status from "In Progress" to "Completed" if the move is confirmed done, since move dates April 14–17 have passed.
- Update the Herrera Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (record: recReloc00000000033) to change status from "In Progress" to "Completed" if the move is confirmed done, since move dates April 14–16 have passed.
- Update the Okonjo Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (record: recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) to change status from "In Progress" to "Completed" if the move is confirmed done, since move dates April 14–18 have passed.
- Update the Kowalski Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (record: recReloc00000000037) to change status from "In Progress" to "Completed" if the move is confirmed done, since move dates April 22–24 have passed.
- Create an Airtable relocation record for Dr. Mehta using airtable_create_record if one does not exist (fields: Name: Dr. Anand Mehta, Company: Canopy Health, Status: In Progress, Origin: Boston MA, Destination: Chicago IL, Move Start Date: 2026-05-02 or 2026-05-03, Move End Date: 2026-05-05, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Special Requirements: executive white-glove move, sensitive medical equipment requiring chain-of-custody).
OE 15: Post a message in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message, tagging Chloe Vance (@moveops_chloe_vance or by name), flagging compliance exposure tied to Fatimah's moves. Content should cover: (1) the PHMSA inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) involves 4 shipments, including Mei-Lin Zhao's Class 9 lithium-ion shipment which Fatimah coordinated — Zhao's documentation is verified complete; (2) the Raj Patel / NorthWind move, where Fatimah's dispatch email confirmed the escort for 9 AM but movers arrived at 7 AM and loaded without the IT escort — a Linear comment flags that the signed chain-of-custody log cannot be located, representing a documentation gap; (3) request confirmation from Chloe on whether any additional action is needed from Fatimah to support the June 5 PHMSA submission deadline.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

3 The follow-up email to Dr. Mehta (visible in send_email parameters) conveys urgency - referencing the May 5 start date, the expired 48-hour hold on the housing units, or the tight timeline for finalizing movers and equipment logistics (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email clearly conveys urgency: references the expired 48-hour holds, the May 2 move-in target ('six days away'), the April 28-30 pack window, and the dependency of Swift logistics on the housing selection. An end-of-day-tomorrow deadline is also given.
✓Run #2The email conveys strong urgency: references that 48-hour holds 'expired yesterday (Friday April 25)', states 'pack date is April 28–30', notes the 'May 5 start is firm', and explicitly says 'every day matters' and that housing is 'the single biggest blocker on the rest of your move logistics.'
✓Run #3The email explicitly mentions 'your May 5 start date at Canopy,' that 'the 48-hour holds that UrbanNest placed on all three units may have expired,' the April 28 pack date being 'only two days away,' and that Swift cannot be locked in without a confirmed address.
✓Run #4The email references urgency including: '48-hour holds expired yesterday (Friday)', 'April 28-30' pack date, 'May 5' start date dependency, and explicit warning that 'every day of delay...compresses the window for the equipment logistics.'
✓Run #5The email conveys urgency by referencing the expired 48-hour holds on the UrbanNest units, the April 28–30 pack date being 'two to four days away,' and that the mover booking and logistics cannot proceed without housing confirmation.
✓Run #6The email body conveys urgency by referencing 'your move date is May 5, which is nine days away', the lapsed 48-hour holds ('The 48-hour holds that UrbanNest placed on these units have now lapsed'), and the dependency on housing for finalizing movers and logistics.

outcome
